supporting_context: Function normalize_email should trim surrounding whitespace and lowercase the domain only.
The API contract says anonymous requests to POST /orders return 401 and valid scoped users return 201.
The user story is that an anonymous user signs in and reaches the authenticated dashboard.
test_snippet:
def test_login_flow_works(page):
    page.goto('/login')
    page.locator('.primary-login').click()
    page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
    assert page.url.endswith('/dashboard')

supporting_context: Password length must be between 6 and 15 characters inclusive; too-short and too-long values are rejected.
DELETE /admin/users/{id} requires an authenticated admin role. The suite covers anonymous and admin success only.
POST /orders validates that sku is required and quantity is between 1 and 10. The test only asserts a successful valid request.
loadProfile rejects with NotFoundError when the profile service returns 404. The test only covers the resolved branch.
The CLI accepts --mode values fast or safe. Any other mode must be rejected with exit code 2.
Discount percentage is clamped to the inclusive range 0..100 and must always stay within bounds.
POST /login has success, invalid password, missing password, and locked-user partitions. The suite covers only successful credentials.
POST /login must distinguish valid, invalid password, missing password, and unauthorized user states.
The /checkout page validates quantity 1..10 on submit and shows a user-visible error for invalid quantities. This E2E test only proves happy navigation.
Logging out must invalidate the session cookie and prevent returning to /dashboard with the old session.
A cookie-backed Server Action requires a CSRF token for POST requests. The test covers only a valid token.
GET /health is documented as a static platform health check delegated to the hosting probe; it returns a constant 204 and has no auth, validation, or state behavior.
